title: Iran Oil Crisis: What Happens Next to Your Bitcoin & Stocks
transcript: Geopolitical  expert  Billy  Bob  Thornton   has  explained  why  we  are  in  an  absolute   do  or  die  moment  for  the  economy  and  the   stress  by  rapidly  increasing  oil  prices   which  this  week  had  their  largest  ever   single  week  increase  on  record  with  a   34.5%   increase  which  on  its  own  almost  spiked   gas  up  to  crisis  levels  not  seen  since   the  Ukraine  war  began  in  February  of   2022.  That's  right.  The  conflict  between   the  United  States  and  Iran  is  forcing   oil  to  the  brink.  Inflation  is  rearing   its  ugly  head.  And  markets  from  the  S&P   500  to  Bitcoin  and  crypto  are  paying  the   price.  So  today,  we're  going to  look  at   what's  happening  in  markets,  what  we  can   expect,  and  how  to  defend  and  plan   around  this  particular  crisis.  Because   the  truth  is  that  crisis  breeds   opportunity.  And  there  is  going  to  be   massive  opportunities  in  markets  if   you're  able  to  game  plan  and  actually   strategize  how  this  will  play  out.   You're  going  to  absolutely  love  this   one.  Make  sure  to  smash  that  like  button   and  subscribe  if  you're  not  already   subscribed  cuz  we're  going  to  be  taking   you  through  the  good,  bad,  and  ugly  of   this  Iranian  conflict  and  talking  about   how  it  impacts  our  beloved  bags.  So,   first  and  foremost,  let's  talk  about  the   market.  One,  oil  is  up  here  at  over  90   bucks  a  gallon,  which  is  where  the  real   pinch  starts  to  come  in  on  the  American   consumer.  This  is  a  massive  amount  of   inflation  in  nearly  everything  at  a  time   where  affordability  is  the  number  one   topic  on  everybody's  mind  when  it  comes   to  the  midterm  election.  We  already  know   that  it's  very  unlikely  for  whoever  wins   the  presidential  election  to  maintain   and  win  the  midterm  election.  It's   almost  never  happened  in  history.  I   think  only  two  times  in  history  has  the   house  or  the  party  that's  won  the   election  actually  gained  any  support  in   the  midterm  election.  It  usually  swings   back.  And  looking  at  what  we're  looking   at  right  now,  it  looks  like  it's  going   to  do  the  same.  And  here's  why.  Oil   through  the  roof.  Meanwhile,  the  S&P  500   looks  like  it's  headed  to  fall  off  a   cliff.  I  mean,  look  at  this  rollover   that  has  been  happening  in  slow  motion   here.  And  as  you  can  see,  this  weekly   candle  was  a  nasty  red  one  now  pushing   below  the  prior  weekly  low  range.  And   you  can  see,  we're  testing  the  prior   weekly  low  from  back  in  December  of   2025.  Uh,  and  below  that  we  have  a   pretty  significant  sell-off  if  we  make   it  down  to  the  next  level,  which  is   another  3%  down,  which  in  S&P  terms  is  a   monster  amount.  And  if  we  lose  that   level,  we  could  go  as  low  as  uh  6,200,   which  would  be  the  next  meaningful  low   at  another  7%  down.  And  of  course,  this   would  be  a  disaster  zone  at  another  23%   down  if  we  go  all  the  way  back  and   retest  the  lows  from  2024.  Meanwhile,  in   other  markets,  the  market  we  care  about   most,  Bitcoin  has  had,  and  this  is  the   weekly  chart.  Let's  just  put  it  out  for   you  real  simple  and  plain.  This  is   exactly  the  pattern  of  death  that  we've   seen  take  place  several  times  before   major  corrections  in  Bitcoin,  where  we   have  this  key  level  of  72K  that  we've   been  watching  now  for  quite  some  time,   which  is  where  the  massive  downtrend   accelerated  from.  And  we've  been  talking   about  getting  back  above  it  now  for   quite  some  time.  And  as  soon  as  we  did,   just  almost  minutes  later,  we  see  the   rejection  back  down  into  the  range,   which  is  exactly  what  happened  here  at   94K  and  exactly  what  happened  up  at  the   top  at  126K.  So  these  moments  right  here   are  typically  how  very  nasty  downtrends   have  started.  And  this  is  actually   happened  several  times  here.  We  can  see   it  happened  right  here  in  2025.  We  can   see  it  happened  right  here  again  before   the  major  collapse.  And  we  can  see  again   it  happened  with  a  swing  failure  pattern   right  here  in  the  beginning  of  2024   before  a  massive  downtrend.  This  is  how   these  downtrends  start  is  they  happen   with  a  swing  failure  on  the  weekly  and   they  get  real  real  nasty.  Now  of  course   past  performance  doesn't  guarantee   future  performance  but  in  the  midst  of  a   bare  market  year  in  the  midst  of  a   four-year  cycle  downturn  from  Bitcoin  in   the  midst  of  a  new  global  conflict   started  in  the  Middle  East  with  one  of   the  strongest  countries  America  has   opposed  in  my  lifetime.  maybe  your   lifetimes.  This  is  a  time  where  we   should  at  least  expect  some  weakness   before  we  see  the  next  piece.  And  when  I   say  next  piece,  I'm  saying  that  there's   usually  a  common  idea  called  buy  the   invasion,  which  means  that  there's  a   sell-off  that  happens  when  a  war  begins.   But  then  very  shortly  after,  the  market   starts  pricing  in  increased  amounts  of   spending  from  the  government  to   effectively  pay  for  the  war.  And  so   maybe  this  will  be  similar.  But  I  want   to  be  very,  very  clear,  the  time  to  buy   the  invasion  isn't  yet.  We  want  to  see   some  freakout  in  the  markets  which  will   then  lead  to  the  government  saying,   "Hey,  we  need  to  stabilize  things  here."   As  Arthur  Hayes  says,  he's  waiting  to   see  a  move  up  in  the  move  index  higher.   And  this  was  a  few  days  ago.  And  then,   wouldn't  you  know  it,  we  have  exactly   the  move  up  in  the  move  index  that  he's   been  looking  for.  Now,  I  want  to  be   clear.  I  have  a  huge  video  coming  out  on   all  of  the  strategic  goals  of  the  Trump   administration,  or  at  least  those  that   we  can  observe  in  why  they  are  going   after  Iran.  and  also  all  of  the  risk   that  this  poses  to  this  administration   and  to  America  in  general.  This  is  going   to  be  a  deep  dive  into  the  relationship   between  every  player  on  the  board  in  the   global  energy  trade  and  it's  going  to  be   one  of my  best  videos.  I'm  very,  very   proud  of  it.  So,  if  you  are  excited,   smash  that  like  button  and  stay  tuned.   But  I'm  going to  give  you  some  key   cliffnotes  because  they  are  critical  to   understanding  how  things  will  be  playing   out  from  a  market  standpoint.  First  and   foremost,  what  you  have  to  know  about   Iran  is  that  it's  not  just  a  significant   amount  of  oil,  but  it's  the  type  of  oil   that  is  in  Iran.  You  see,  Iran  has   something  that  they  call  sweet  crude.  It   is  a  totally  different  molecular  makeup   from  what  is  in  the  USA  oil  or   Venezuelan  oil.  For  example,  Iranian  oil   is  very  easy  to  distill  into  gasoline  or   different  petroleum  products  and  you  can   make  almost  any  petroleum  product  from   it.  Whereas  USA  oil  is  more  difficult   and  Venezuelan  oil  is  very  difficult.  So   just  because  it's  oil  doesn't  mean  one   barrel  of  oil  equals  one  barrel  of  oil.   In  fact,  they  are  all  very  different   products.  And  Iranian  oil  or  the  Gulf   oil  that  is  in  Saudi  Arabia,  Iran,  and   the  Middle  East  tends  to  be  the  very   very  high  quality  sweet  crude  that   actually  is  responsible  for  a   significantly  bigger  impact  on  the   global  energy  trade  than  just  the  amount   of  barrels  going  out  there  in  the  world.   Not  all  barrels  of  oil  are  created   equal.  It's  very  important  you   understand  this.  When  Iran  shuts  down   this  straight  of  Hormuz,  stopping  these   tankers  from  going  back  and  forth  to   deliver  their  cargo,  it  has  a  massive   effect  not  just  on  Iran,  but  on  China,   on  India,  on  the  entire  world.  Now,  some   are  concluding  that  this  is  the  new   Breton  Woods,  effectively  the  biggest   and  most  dangerous  geopolitical  blitz   since  Breton  Woods.  Now,  Bretton  Woods   solidified  that  the  whole  world  would   trade  with  the  US  dollar  on  the  back  of   the  energy  trade.  And  that's  why  they   call  it  the  petro  dollar.  If  you've  ever   heard  that  expression,  it  means  that  the   US  dollar  is  given  its  value  by  the  fact   that  everyone  needs  oil  and  you  need   dollars  to  get  that  oil.  This  petro   dollar  thing  emerged  over  50  years  ago   at  Breton  Woods.  And  now  this  is  the   reshaping  or  the  next  chapter  in  this   that  is  extremely  extremely  risky.  Okay?   And  that's  because  all  of  the  seemingly   erratic,  seemingly  random  actions  that   Trump  has  taken  from  the  tariffs,  which   are  being  used  as  both  a  carrot  and  a   stick  for  trading  partners,  China   included,  as  well  as  taking  over   Venezuela,  focusing  on  controlling   Canada  and  their  resources,  now  Iran.   And  finally,  we  have  Russia.  The  endgame   is  that  bricks,  the  Brazil,  Russia,   India,  China,  and  South  Africa  alliance   that  was  designed  to  destabilize  the  US   dollar  influence  will  effectively  lose   its  economic  foundation  and  Beijing  will   be  cut  off  from  reliable  energy  and   geopolitical  allies.  So,  it's  not  just   about  replacing  the  very  brutal  Islamic   regime  in  Iran  with  a  more   Westernfriendly  democracy,  though  that   is  a  benefit  here.  Every  last  drop  of   this  is  about  countering  China.  And   almost  everything  Trump  has  done  since   he  took  office  in  January  of  2025  has   been  about  China.  The  tariffs,  the   invasions,  the  threats,  the  back  and   forth  on  various  topics,  the  focus  on   AI,  even  the  focus  on  cryptocurrency  he   views  as  we  have  to  do  it  or  China  will   do  it.  Every  single  thing  is  about  the   USA  versus  their  biggest  global   geopolitical  nemesis  in  China.  Now,  I   will  say  we  have  much  more  analysis  on   how  this  is  going  to  play  out.  I  highly   suggest  that  you  tune  in  for  that  next   video.  But  now,  let's  talk  about  the   actual  way  this  is  affecting  markets   because  markets  do  not  like  this.  They   do  not  like  the  uncertainty  with  markets   that  were  already  feeling  weak.  And  the   question  is,  how  is  this  going  to  play   out?  Now,  there  was  an  amazing  thread   here  by  the  Kobiosi  letter  where  they   say  this  is  their  conflict  playbook   based  on  how  Trump  tends  to  go  through   his  more  aggressive  and  erratic  policy   maneuvers.  Well,  he  effectively  has  a  2   to  four  week  timeline  in  effect.  Now,   this  original  tweet  says  that  there  are   several  stages  to  this  where  the  first   step  is  every  conflict  starts  the  same   way.  He  hits  them  with  the  military   first  with  a  very  threatening  military   action.  He  did  this  with  Iran.  He's  done   this  with  Venezuela  by  pinning  down   their  airspace.  And  then  he  also  did   this  for  Greenland.  He  comes  in  with  the   stick  first.  Very  intimidating.  Then  he   uh  strategically  postures  and  physically   postures.  This  is  followed  by  a   nighttime  action.  In  the  case  of   Venezuela,  it  was  pulling  Maduro  right   out  of  his  bed.  And  in  the  case  of  Iran,   it  has  been  these  sudden  strikes.  Then   effectively,  you  see  risk  premiums  go  up   because  there's  a  ton  of  uncertainty.   Nobody  knows  what's  going  to  happen   next.  And  the  market  starts  pricing  in   risk.  The  VIX  goes  up.  But  the  big  thing   is  that  Trump  needs  to  be  taken   seriously.  If  people  don't  believe  he's   serious  about  waging  a  long-term   conflict,  then  he  has  no  leverage.  If   they  think  this  is  just  going  to  be  over   in  a  couple  of  days,  then  they'll  just   hunker  down  and  they  won't  give  any   meaningful  concession.  So  he'll  always   tease  this  forever  conflict  and  he'll   always  come  off  like  he's  willing  to   drop  an  NUK  or  drop  something  crazy   because  that's  how  he  gets  his  leverage   by  being  seen  as  the  crazy  guy  who  is   willing  to  do  crazy  things.  And  that   fear  is  part  of  his  strategy.  We've  seen   it  too  many  times  for  it  to  be  a   coincidence.  So  then  we  get  this  phase   which  is  that  markets  are  starting  to   price  in  a  prolonged  conflict.  And  this   is  when  you  see  oil  go  through  the  roof.   You  see  stocks  start  to  sell  off  and  you   start  to  see  the  market  enter  into  the   fear  phase.  That  is  exactly  where  we  are   right  now  is  the  fear  phase  where   markets  are  saying,  "Wait  a  second.  Is   this  going  to  go  on  forever?  How  long  is   this  going  to  last?  And  how  big  of  a   risk  is  this  going  to  be?  Is  there  a   risk  America  could  overextend?  Could   allies  start  flipping  on  America?  Are   the  Gulf  States  going  to  betray  America   and  flip  on  them  as  we're  hearing  right   now?  We're  hearing  about  the  tension   between  the  UAE  and  Saudi  and  how  they   no  longer  want  to  do  business  with   America  and  they're  putting  their   pressure  on  the  US.  Meanwhile,  Trump  is   teasing  the  idea  of  a  ground  war  and   actually  sending  in  tens  of  thousands  or   more  American  troops,  boots  on  the   ground  into  Iran.  And  this  is  where  you   get  the  magic  opportunity,  right?  This   is  where  the  buy  the  invasion  concept   comes  from.  It's  not  about  just  buying   the  first  news  of  conflict.  You're  not   buying  the  air  strike.  You  want  to   actually  understand  and  hopefully  invest   before  the  conditional  deescalation   signal  appears.  Right?  This  is  where  you   get  the  taco  that  Trump  always  chickens   out.  and  maybe  he's  not  chickening  out   if  he  ends  up  getting  his  demands  met.   And  then  finally,  you  have  a  feedback   loop  followed  by  the  deal  and  narrative   framing.  This  is  when  the  markets  will   start  to  rejoice  here.  And  then  we  get  a   violent  repricing  and  political  victory   lap.  And  this  uh  poster,  Kobayasi   believes  this  could  happen  in  two  to   four  weeks.  Now,  I  want  to  be  clear,   this  is  where  the  concept  of  buying  the   invasion  comes  from.  And  this  is  where   Arthur  Hayes's  article  where  he  was   talking  about  waiting  for  the  government   to  start  indicating  that  they're  willing   to  finance  this  ongoing  conflict  in   Iran.  So  you  have  two  triggers  here.  You   either  have  the  chickening  out  or  the   taco  trade  by  Trump  where  he  pulls  out   of  Iran  or  essentially  signals  that   there's  a  deal  on  the  table  or  you  wait   for  the  government  itself  to  indicate   that  they're  willing  to  do  extra  things   to  finance  the  war.  Whether  that's   taking  on  debt,  printing  money,  or   coming  up  with  some  new  four-letter   acronym  for  a  new  term  that  we've  never   heard  of,  which  effectively  translates   to  mo  money.  And  so,  amidst  all  this,   we've  now  seen  that  the  United  States   wants  to  counter  China.  One  thing  that   we  know  that  they  don't  want  is  for   Russia  and  China  to  start  teaming  up  and   countering  America.  They  do  not  like   that.  So,  it  was  a  very  interesting,   perhaps  super  unexpected  piece  of  news   that  the  US  is  going  to  unsanction   unsanction  Russian  oil.  Now,  they're   doing  this  for  two  reasons.  The  first  is   that  the  world  needs  oil  and  it  needs  it   now.  It  cannot  wait  months  and  months   for  this  to  work  itself  out.  The  world   grinds  to  a  halt  without  oil.  And  one  of   our  close  friends,  India,  is  in  trouble.   They  really  need  oil  right  now.  And   without  the  straight  of  Hormuz  being   opened,  the  only  way  for  us  to  really   get  India  the  oil  they  need  is  by   tapping  into  one  of  the  biggest  oil   producers,  Russia,  who  has  some  oil   already  in  the  region.  And  so  the   concept  here  is  let  India  buy  some  oil   from  Russia  of  course  in  US  dollars  but   on  top  of  that  this  now  becomes  a   strategic  advantage  for  Russia.  All  of  a   sudden  now  this  Iranian  conflict  isn't   the  US  encroaching  into  Russian   territory  which  we  know  Iran  and  Russia   and  China  are  all  fairly  good  friends.   This  isn't  just  the  US  taking  something   from  Russia.  This  is  actually  the  US   kind  of  giving  a  feather  in  the  cap  to   Putin  which  now  drives  a  wedge   strategically  and  geopolitically  between   Russia  and  China.  They're  no  longer  in   the  same  boat,  in  the  same  straight  of   hormuz.  They're  now  actually   experiencing  very  different  strategic   trade-offs.  And  that  is  actually,  dare  I   say,  a  very  intelligent  move  by  this   administration.  Again,  I  want  to  be   clear.  Nobody's  saying  Putin's  a  good   guy.  Nobody's  saying  that  we  approve  of   anything  that  the  Russians  do.  Not   saying  that.  I'm  saying  that  if  the   United  States  wants  to  be  successful,   they  want  to  split  Russia  and  China   apart.  And  they  want  to  counter  China's   influence.  That  is  the  goal.  The  very   obvious  goals  of  all  these  actions  is   that  it  is  a  geopolitical  chess  match   against  China.  So  the  question  is  what   would  help  do  that?  And  I  want  to  be   very  very  clear.  Obviously  I  have  been   supportive  of  Trump  specifically  his   crypto  agenda  here  on  the  channel.  But   as  far  as  wars,  I'm  not  an  expert  in  my   lifetime.  I've  never  seen  a  war  end  up   doing  all  these  great  things  for   America.  I'm  a  millennial  that  has  seen   mostly  the  forever  wars  of  the  early   2000s  and  was  deeply  deeply  always   opposed  to  war.  So  that's  still  my   worldview.  Now  that  we're  in  this   conflict  though,  I  want  to  first   understand  what  the  heck's  going  on,  and   secondly,  I  want  to  understand  what  the   best  version  of  this  for  the  United   States  is  because,  hey,  that's  what  I   want  to  be  rooting  for,  the  quickest   ending  with  the  most  strategic   advantages  for  the  country  in  which  I   live.  I  don't  think  that's  crazy.  But  on   top  of  that,  I  just  want  to  be  clear   here  that  I'm  trying  to  outline  how  this   affects  the  markets.  And  sometimes  it   may  sound  like  I'm  describing  things  as   good  or  bad.  That's  not  really  my   intention.  I  want  to  synthesize  the   information  and  deliver  it  to  you  so  you   understand  what  I'm  thinking.  And  so   some  people  are  actually  believing  that   Beijing  did  not  see  this  coming  with  the   Iranian  invasion  here  and  that  we  can   actually  observe  that  they  might  be  a   little  scared  because  they've  done  no   flights  at  all  around  Taiwan,  no   military  drills  around  Taiwan  at  all,   which  they've  been  doing  with   regularity.  This  is  very  irregular,   which  maybe  means  that  China  might  be  a   little  bit  freaked  out  by  all  of  these   moves  that  the  US  is  doing.  Maybe  they   didn't  see  this  coming.  And  so  we  may  be   seeing  that  leverage  is  being  built  up   by  the  United  States.  It's  too  early  to   say,  too  hard  to  call.  However,  it  is   possible  that  if  this  comes  to  a  close   in  a  relatively  short  amount  of  time   that  this  would  have  been  a  major  major   strategic  victory  against  China  and  this   whole  bricks  alliance  that  is  very   anti-American.  It's  important  to  realize   that  Trump  has  a  timing  issue  here.   Okay.  As  oil  spikes  up,  so  does   inflation  and  we  already  have  an   affordability  crisis  and  we're  seeing   cracks  across  the  rest  of  the  economy.   In  this  case,  we're  seeing  Black   Rockck's  $26  billion  private  credit  fund   is  limiting  how  much  investors  can  pull   out,  capping  withdrawals  at  5%  even   though  investors  asked  for  9.3%.   Blackstone's  similar  fund  processed  a   record  of  7.9%  of  shares  and  they   actually  needed  employees  covering  along   with  the  firm's  own  capital.  So,  what   we're  seeing  here  is  that  there  is   stress  in  private  credit  markets.   Private  credit  is  where  essentially   people  lend  to  companies.  You  say,   "Okay,  well,  I  actually  believe   Microsoft  is  a  great  company.  So,  I'll   lend  them  money.  I  don't  think  there's   any  chance  that  they're  going to  go   bankrupt,  but  you'll  do  it  at  a  slightly   higher  interest  rate  than  you  would   actually  lend  your  money,  say,  to  the  US   government,  which  is  almost  impossible   for  it  to  go  under  because  they  can   print  money.  So,  you  lend  to  these   companies  at  a  slightly  higher  rate,  but   it's  generally  pretty  safe,  especially   if  you're  in  high  quality  private   credit.  But  seeing  this,  seeing  this   fund  effectively  saying,  "No,  no,  no,   no,  no.  We're  not  going  to  let  you  get   your  money  out  is  extremely,  extremely   concerning.  And  it  shows  that  there's  a   pinch  right  now  on  the  economy  and  that   private  credit  is  potentially  the  source   of  a  black  swan  if  there  is  one  coming.   Okay,  this  is  what  we  have  to  be  on  huge   high  alert  with  because  in  order  for  the   government  to  really  step  in  and  say,   you  know  what,  we're  going  to  start   printing  money.  We're  going  to  go  into   actual,  you  know,  damage  control  mode   like  they  did  in  the  great  financial   crisis  like  they  did  in  2020,  there   needs  to  be  an  impetus,  a  huge   crisis-like  moment  which  sets  these   things  in  motion.  And  it's  not  just,   hey,  jobs  are  a  little  slow,  or  hey,   there's  a  little  stress.  There  needs  to   be  a  crisis  of  some  sort.  And  so,  when   you  see  stuff  like  this  out  of  one  of   the  world's  biggest  investment  banks,   Black  Rockck,  if  not  the  biggest,  this   is  a  signal.  This  is  a  red  flag  that   we're  seeing  stress  on  the  economy  and   there  are  cracks  in  the  armor.  Add  to   this  that  there's  also  apparently  a   labor  market  myth  that  just  broke.  We've   been  hearing  about  the  economy   reacelerating,  tons  of  reaceleration  in   the  economy.  Well,  after  February's   minus  92,000  print  and  nearly  700,000  in   downward  revisions  over  the  past  12   months,  well,  turns  out  there  isn't   really  that  strong  of  an  economy.  There   isn't  that  strong  labor  market  that   we've  been  hearing  about  from  the  Fed.   In  fact,  the  labor  market  might  not  be   strong  at  all.  Add  to  this  that  we  see   that  white  collar  work,  knowledge  work,   is  going  to  be  displaced  in  some   capacity  at  some  time  in  the  not  too   distant  future.  Whether  you  believe  it's   going  to  be  a  massive  tsunami  that  wipes   everyone  out,  or  whether  you  believe   that  it's  going  to  be  controlled  and   slow  and  sudden,  whatever  you  believe   in,  there's  some  effect  now  where   knowledge  workers,  the  most  highly  paid,   high  spending  types  of  people  who  have   highquality,  very  expensive  mortgages   and  debt  to  pay  back,  well,  those   people's  jobs  are  the  most  threatened.   So  we  see  here  uh  Anthropic  has  just   published  a  real  exposure  report  here   where  you  can  see  that  you  know  office   and  admin,  legal,  arts  and  media,   education,  social  services,  life  and   social  sciences,  management,  business   and  finance,  computer  and  math,   architecture  and  engineering.  These   fields  are  at  extreme  risk  of  being   disrupted.  And  it  doesn't  matter  whether   you're  not  a  lawyer  or  you're  not  a   doctor.  If  all  them  stop  paying  their   rents  and  all  of  them  can't  afford  their   payments  on  their  mortgages  and  stop   going  out  and  buying  Christmas  gifts  or,   you  know,  Valentine's  Day  gifts,  well,   guess  what?  The  economy  grinds  to  a  halt   and  we  get  not  a  recession.  We  get  a  new   and  absolutely  insane  type  of   depression.  So,  there  are  many  storms  on   the  horizon.  And  the  question  is,  when   do  we  see  something  break?  Because  right   now,  it  seems  a  lot  more  like  either   we'll  get  a  swift  resolution  to  this   conflict  where  everything  resumes  to   normal,  we  get  a  bullish  resumption.   That  would  be  an  opportunity  when  we   start  seeing  that  conflict  resolve  or   just  as  likely  we  might  see  something   actually  break.  These  are  the  types  of   conditions  in  cyclical  downtrends  with   the  S&P  overextended  and  rolling  over   with  Bitcoin  already  in  a  brutal  bare   market  with  liquidity  already  scared   with  AI  dispossessing  jobs  with  the   labor  market  coming  in  and  revising   everything  down.  We  see  the  opportunity   for  a  genuine  moment  of  weakness  during   which  I  believe  there  would  be  true   panic.  And  that,  my  friends,  will  be  the   opportunity.  That  will  be  when  we  jump   in  and  we  buy  before  the  government   steps  in  with  the  money  printer  or  right   after  they  step  in  with  the  money   printer.  It  doesn't  matter.  You  don't   have  to  be  a  hero.  But  you  wait.  You   wait  for  the  signal  that  the  monetary   conditions  and  the  monetary  supply  will   start  to  change.  And  that  is  the  time  to   ape  in  like  there's  no  other  into   Bitcoin,  into  good  cryptos,  and  into   risk  assets  in  general  that  will  be   reflexive  to  the  upside  as  they  print   money.  Now,  as  far  as  great  coins,   obviously,  Hyperlquid  has  been   absolutely  central,  helping  actually   create  price  discovery  on  the  weekends   for  assets  like  gold,  like  oil.  If   you're  trading  on  Hyperlid,  you  get  to   trade  while  the  markets  are  closed.   Right  now,  it's  a  Friday  evening  and  the   traditional  markets  are  closed.  Well,   guess  what's  not  closed?  Guess  where  the   price  discovery  is  still  happening?  Is   on  Hyperlid,  which  is  not  a  small  and   insignificant  detail.  This  is  providing   a  genuine  service  of  price  discovery  and   informing  how  markets  will  work  globally   during  the  times  when  markets  are  closed   in  the  traditional  sphere.  So  hyperlid   becomes  very  interesting  not  just  in   this  dynamic  but  again  as  we're  looking   for  this  moment  of  crisis  and  trying  to   figure  out  what  we  might  buy.  Hype  is   certainly  something  that  I  think  starts   to  make  more  and  more  sense  over  time.   And  I  personally  will  be  looking  to   scoop  some  up  if  we  do  get  this  moment   of  crisis  of  weakness.  I  think  hype  is   proving  itself  out  as  one  of  the  best   things  that's  ever  happened  in  crypto.  I   also  thought  this  was  absolutely   hilarious.  So,  Calidora  here  is  the  uh   founder  of  OTM  Labs,  which  is  a  uh  real   world  assets  platform.  And  check  this   out.  The  New  York  Stock  Exchange   invested  in  OKX  at  a  $25  billion   valuation.  The  token  FDV  is  $  1.6   billion.  Barely  pumped.  Another  example   of  how  token  holders  not  getting  what   they  deserve.  tokens  need  to  become   onchain  equity  ASAP  or  we're  doomed.  And   uh  Calidora  says  crypto  reinventing   securities  from  first  principles,  which   is  a  very  very  funny  thing  to  say   because  if  you  think  about  it,  crypto   was  all  about,  oh  no,  we  don't  want  all   these  rules.  We  don't  want  all  these,   you  know,  uh  shackles  on  the  creators  of   these  assets.  You  should  just  be  able  to   create  networks  and  let  the  you  know,   open  source  code  run  everything.  The   problem  is  that  the  way  that  crypto  has   evolved,  there's  often  times  a  private   company  as  well  as  a  public  token,  and   the  token  doesn't  have  any  of  the  rights   or  inclusions  that  the  private  company   might  have.  For  example,  the  OKX  token   here,  OKB,  yes,  it  benefits  from  some  of   their  revenues  and  their  buybacks,  but   they  could  sell  this  company  to  the  New   York  Stock  Exchange  for  $28  billion  or   whatever  their  valuation  here  is,  and   they  wouldn't  owe  the  token  holders   anything.  And  that's  where  you  see  that   over  time  the  community  will  demand   those  rights  from  the  tokens  and  we'll   get  once  again  back  to  securities.   Something  like  poetic  about  that  that   you  end  up  with  this  whole  new  system   that  has  no  rules,  no  responsibilities.   You  get  to  create  something  brand  new   from  scratch  and  you  end  up  right  back   where  you  were  because  there's  a  reason   why  those  things  existed  in  the  first   place.  Fate  loves  a  little  bit  of  poetic   justice.  Anyway,  to  be  clear,  I  am  on   guard. summary_hu: Billy Bob Thornton geopolitikai elemző felhívja a figyelmet arra, hogy egy kritikus gazdasági helyzetben vagyunk, amelyet az olajár drámai emelkedése okoz. Az olajár heti 34,5%-os növekedése a legmagasabb a nyilvántartás szerint. Az infláció megugrása már most nyomás alá helyezi a piacokat, a részvényektől kezdve a kriptovalutákig. A S&P 500 várhatóan jelentős veszteségeket szenvedhet el, míg a Bitcoin technikai mintázata a közelgő korrigálásra utal. Thornton hangsúlyozza, hogy az iráni konfliktus potenciálisan óriási lehetőségeket kínál, ha megfelelő stratégiát alakítanak ki a befektetők. Az olajminőség különbsége is központi szerepet játszik a globális energiakereskedelemben, mivel az iráni olaj kiemelkedő, 'édes' típusú. A geopolitikai feszültségek és a globális piacok instabilitása miatt késlekedni kell a tőkebefektetésekkel, amíg a helyzet le nem csillapodik.
summary_en: Geopolitical expert Billy Bob Thornton indicates that we are in a critical economic moment driven by a dramatic surge in oil prices, with a record increase of 34.5% in a single week. This inflationary pressure is already impacting markets from stocks to cryptocurrencies. The S&P 500 is projected to face significant losses, while Bitcoin's technical patterns suggest a forthcoming correction. Thornton emphasizes that the Iranian conflict may present massive opportunities for investors if they develop appropriate strategies. The distinction in oil quality also plays a central role in global energy trade, as Iranian oil is high-quality 'sweet' crude. Given the geopolitical tensions and market instabilities, investors must delay significant investments until the situation stabilizes.
key_points_hu: A közeli olajár-emelkedés inflációs nyomást gyakorol az amerikai fogyasztókra.
A globális piacon a Bitcoin technikai mintázatai korrekcióra utalnak.
Az iráni konfliktus potenciálisan új lehetőségeket kínál a befektetők számára.
key_points_en: The recent surge in oil prices exerts inflationary pressure on American consumers.
Bitcoin's technical patterns in the global market suggest a correction is imminent.
The Iranian conflict may potentially offer new opportunities for investors.
main_topics: Geopolitical implications of the Iranian oil crisis
